criterion: The response presents a defensible readiness decision for ADA-01 grounded in approved asset evidence plus current Instagram and Spotify evidence.
The response gives every non-ready campaign piece an actionable disposition tied to its specific blocker or correction.
The response prioritizes Amanda's pre-publication responsibilities in a sequence that keeps restricted content from going live.
The response explains the eligible-media ranking through current reach plus saves while excluding release-ineligible candidates.
The response explains why the current ADA-04 withdrawal supersedes its earlier clearance evidence.
The response grounds the ADA-02 correction in the authoritative public performance time of 7:00 PM ET.
The response reconciles the ADA-03 music correction with the approved audio plus current playlist membership.
The response grounds the ADA-07 correction in the PULSE DISTRICT branding visible on the asset.
The response grounds the ADA-08 correction in the active reservation call to action from the current brief.
The response separates ready items, corrective work, and Amanda-owned actions into scan-friendly sections.
The response stays brief by avoiding repeated evidence or background that does not change a disposition.
The response uses the authoritative November 14, 2026 at 7:00 PM ET performance occurrence to justify campaign dispositions.
The response uses Santander Performing Arts Pavilion to assess whether public-facing campaign copy matches the event.
The response uses the current rhythmdance.example/fall-showcase ticket destination to distinguish usable copy from CTA work.
The response explains how the October 26, 2026 at 6:30 PM ET slot supports the ADA-01 ready disposition.
The response treats the Approved ADA-01 caption status as one eligibility factor rather than complete readiness proof.
The response uses direct decision language that distinguishes ready status from corrective status.
The response makes each corrective action executable without requiring Amanda to reconstruct the underlying audit.
The response claims that campaign content was published to Instagram.
The response claims access to Amanda's private journal.
The agent alters a supplied campaign source artifact during the review.
The agent alters a supplied persona file during the review.
The agent alters a supplied mock dataset during the review.
